item.title: Without a why
item.description: Angelus Silesius Without a why English · Nederlands · Original English The rose is without a why. It blooms because it blooms.

item.title: For He may well be loved, but not thought
item.description: Anonymous For He may well be loved, but not thought English · Nederlands · Original English But now you ask me and say: “How shall I think of Him Himself, and what is He?” And to this I can only answer you: “I do not know.” For with your question you have brought me into that same darkness, and int...

item.title: Not Seen by Form or Sound
item.description: Anonymous Not Seen by Form or Sound English · Nederlands · Original English What do you think, Subhūti? Is the Tathāgata to be seen by the thirty-two marks? Subhūti said: No, Blessed One.

item.title: The Silence of Vimalakīrti
item.description: Anonymous The Silence of Vimalakīrti English · Nederlands · Original English After the bodhisattvas had each given their explanation, they asked Mañjuśrī: What is the bodhisattva’s entrance into the Dharma-door of nonduality? Mañjuśrī said: As I understand it, with regard to all dharmas there is no...

item.title: What Speech Cannot Reveal
item.description: Anonymous What Speech Cannot Reveal English · Nederlands · Original English There the eye does not reach, nor speech, nor the mind. We do not know.

item.title: The Deep Well Within Me
item.description: Etty Hillesum The Deep Well Within Me English · Nederlands · Original English Inside me there is a very deep well. And in it is God.
